stemHtml: During the climb speed schedule, what is the significance of the "cross over altitude"?"

options.a: The cross over altitude is the only pressure altitude where a certain IAS/CAS matches the same Mach number in any atmospheric condition.
options.b: During climb with a constant speed, a constant Mach number is changed to a constant IAS/CAS at this altitude.
options.c: During climb with a constant speed, a constant IAS/CAS is changed to a constant Mach number at this altitude.
options.d: Climb with a constant IAS/CAS has to be levelled off to achieve a given Mach number at this altitude.
